title: melaleuca fulgens subsp. corrugata
intro: Melaleuca fulgens subsp. corrugata, also known as the paperbark tea tree, is a subspecies of the melaleuca tree, native to Australia and known for its distinctive papery bark. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its essential oils rich in compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol. The health benefits of M. fulgens subsp. corrugata include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as wound healing and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and antifungal properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
